recently_updated.summary: Second correction pass same day (candidate-sourced, submitted by Nixon): (1) QB6_C Q3 — answer was mismatched to the question; replaced with the correct purifier manual desludging procedure (previous answer described a disc-stack overhaul instead). (2) GFI/FuelEU conflation fixed across QB6_E Q2, QB7_A Q3, and QB7_A_CheatSheet: GFI (Greenhouse Gas Fuel Intensity) is the IMO's own Net-Zero Framework metric (93.3 gCO2eq/MJ, 2008 baseline, MEPC 83 / new MARPOL Annex VI Chapter 5) and is a separate regulatory track from FuelEU Maritime's Greenhouse Gas Intensity metric (91.16 gCO2eq/MJ, 2020 baseline, EU Regulation 2023/1805). Prior content wrongly applied the 'GFI' label and/or the IMO 93.3 baseline to FuelEU Maritime, or vice versa. Both tracks now clearly separated with correct baselines, reference years, and reduction schedules attributed to the right regulation. Verified against IMO MEPC 83 official summaries and Global Maritime Forum guidance. QB7_A_CheatSheet untracked in this manifest (cheat sheets not indexed here).

Correction pass (candidate feedback via Nixon/Rathesh): (1) International Group of P&I Clubs member count corrected 13→12 across all affected files, reflecting the North of England P&I / Standard Club merger into NorthStandard (effective 20 Feb 2023) — corrected in QB1_A (Q1, Q29), QB9_C (Q3, Q6), QB9_D (Q1, Q2, plus a duplicate-entry bug in the club roster list), and QB9_D_CheatSheet (untracked in this manifest). (2) IG reinsurance programme figures updated for the 2026/27 policy year: market GXL layer raised to USD 2.35bn, Collective Overspill adds a further USD 1bn, total USD 3.35bn (previously stated as USD 3.1bn) — same files as above. (3) Continuous Synopsis Record (CSR) SOLAS citation corrected: QB1_B Q5 was citing Chapter XI-2 (security chapter) instead of the correct Chapter XI-1, Regulation 5 (Special Measures to Enhance Maritime Safety); QB4_I Q1 was citing 'SOLAS Ch I' instead of Chapter XI-1, Reg 5. Both fixed. All files health-checked (HTML tag balance, gate script integrity) before push; version footers dated and reasoned inline.

Correction pass: QB6_G new Q3 (engine tech retrofit options), QB7_E Q1 CII/WTW causal-error fix, QB4_G Q8 IEEC/COR addition, QB3_C IMO Resolution A.1185(33)→A.1206(34) citation fix. QB2_E JS toggle bug + SOLAS III/rescue-boat citation fix. QB2_G same rescue-boat citation fix (III/14, LSA §6.1.1.11).

recently_updated.note: PSC appeal-chain correction cascade, triggered by a subscriber correction report (Rathesh) on QB4_E Q8. Root cause: the appeal escalation chain's Stage 3 review deadline was stated platform-wide as '120 days from the ship's release from detention' and implicitly presented as flowing from IMO Resolution A.1206(34) itself. Independent verification against the actual A.1206(34) text confirmed it grants only a general, one-line right of appeal (para 2.3.11) with no numeric deadline or staged mechanism specified at IMO level -- the 3-stage chain and day-count are regional MOU procedure. The 120-day figure is correct only for Paris MOU (measured from date of release); IOMOU -- the regime governing Indian ports and the one relevant for Kochi MMD candidates -- specifies 90 days from the date of detention, verified against IOMOU's own 'Detention Review Panel' procedure document (Section 3.13), with a 3-member review panel (not Paris MOU's 4-member panel). This superseded the same-day entry above where QB4_A Q6 and QB4_E Q8 were logged with the then-uncaught 120-day figure. Corrected in: QB4_A Q6 (deadline + panel size), QB4_E Q8 (deadline + panel size + resolution-vs-MOU attribution), and QB4_C Q11 (separately discovered during the audit: governing resolution was still citing the superseded A.1185(33) instead of A.1206(34) throughout -- action-taken code content itself was already correct and unchanged). All three questions now cross-link to each other (appeal chain <-> action codes <-> IOMOU/Paris MOU deadline comparison). Audited all 78 live QB files for the same 120-day/A.1185(33) pattern -- QB3_C, QB4_I, QB9_F matched only on unrelated generic 'detention' text, no further instances found. Health-checked (tag balance, JS syntax, mandatory block counts, structural parity vs originals) before this manifest update; known_traps.md scanner flags 'A.1185(33)' in all three files as an expected false positive -- the mentions are intentional, explaining the superseded resolution for exam-trap awareness, not errors.

Multi-file correction pass. (1) HNS Convention entry-into-force updated from not yet in force to the confirmed date of 29 November 2027 (all conditions met 29 May 2026), plus Tier 1/Tier 2 liability limits (100M/115M SDR shipowner; 250M SDR combined Fund cap) added - corrected in QB1_A Q6 (+ both mental-map diagrams), QB1_B Q1, and QB9_A Q3. (2) MEPC.398(83) NOx Technical Code amendment (certification of engines subject to substantial modification, in force 1 Sep 2026) added to QB1_supplementary Q7. (3) QB4_A Q13: clarified PDSD (STCW A-VI/6-2) and STSDSS are the same requirement, not separate certificates. (4) QB4_A Q6: PSC detention appeal procedure expanded from a single step to the correct 3-stage chain - national appeal to Port State, then Flag State/RO reconsideration request, then MoU Secretariat Review Panel (Flag State/RO request only, 120-day window). (5) QB4_E Q8: corrected superseded IMO Resolution A.1185(33) citation to the current A.1206(34) (Procedures for PSC, 2025, in force 1 Jan 2026); applied the same 3-stage appeal chain correction as QB4_A Q6. (6) QB9_F Q7: replaced a fabricated Ship-to-Shore Data Security System definition of STSDSS with the correct STCW A-VI/6-2 (PDSD) definition - Security Training for Seafarers with Designated Security Duties. All files auth-gated (corrections to already-live content); each corrected question carries a dated version-footer note.

Added Q4 to QB4_D — interdepartmental ISM audit substitution (Master/C.E. cross-swap) during COVID force majeure. Sourced from a Kochi MMD candidate WhatsApp report (Rathesh). Verified against ISM Code §12.1/§12.5 (exact wording quoted), MSC.273(85) 15-month interval ceiling, MSC-MEPC.7/Circ.8, and flag-specific COVID interim policies (Vanuatu, RMI, Liberia, Bahamas). Distinguished from DG Shipping India's actual COVID response, which used the timeline-extension route (DGS Order 06/2020, 13/2020) rather than interdepartmental substitution — a distinction not commonly known among Kochi candidates. Health-checked clean (tag balance, GA4, robots meta, mandatory blocks, q-id sequence, TOC anchors). Gated and ready to push.

MS Act 2025 correction pass. Corrected 30+ questions across 14 files that were still citing the repealed Merchant Shipping Act, 1958 (or treating the 2025 Act as a draft bill). Key fixes: seafarer welfare (Part VII->Part V), CoC/STCW certification (old Part VI Sec 78/79/87 -> Part IV Sec 45/46/48/53), cabotage/coasting trade (old Sec 406/407 -> standalone Coastal Shipping Act 2025), vessel registration (old Sec 21/24-46 -> Part III Sec 14-44), official log book (old Part VII -> Sec 112 Part V), class-suspension penalties (old Sec 334 -> Part VI Sec 125/127), LLMC/CLC/Bunker/Nairobi domestic implementation (old Part XB/XIII -> Part IX/XII), and DG Shipping renamed DGMA throughout. QB9_E Q2 and QB9_F Q6 required full rewrites (previously described the Act as pending and cabotage as still under the MS Act). All files health-checked before this manifest update; not yet re-gated pending Nixon's review.

New file QB10_B (8Q) built — SOLAS/MARPOL amendment cycle 2024-2028, STCW amendments, MLC 2006 amendments, ship-type-specific amendments (container/tanker/bulk), IMO Net-Zero Framework status, and DG Shipping/DGMA 2026 circulars. All resolution numbers independently verified against primary IMO/class sources across multiple review passes (corrected MSC.532(107) inclinometer citation, MSC.520(106) flashpoint citation, MSC.549(108) ETA citation, DGMA naming transition). Originally built as QB10_A but renamed after discovering that filename was already live on GitHub with unrelated content (Sagarmala/Amrit Kaal/OPEC/ship-type basics) — confirmed via direct GitHub raw fetch before renaming, per established QB1_J/QB3_I precedent. Gated after Nixon's review.

New file QB7_I (9Q) added — Latest Onboard Technology/Alternative Fuels/Decarbonisation. Verified/corrected against live sources (VTT MOC study citation, FuelEU penalty escalation figures, MAN ME-GA Nov-2024 discontinuation, EGRBP Jan-2024 nuance). Gated + data-kw SEO added. QB1_J and QB3_I already present in manifest, confirmed unchanged.

Re-verified QB4_C against live GitHub file after Nixon reported a rebuild/push in another session. Question texts (17), order, tags, and Q3/Q11 corrections (SOLAS XI reg numbers, PSC action codes) all confirmed unchanged and correctly reflected in this manifest - no content update needed. However, manifest version field was stale/wrong (showed '1.2'); corrected to '1.1' to match the live page's actual highest card version (header badge shows v1.0, but Q3 and Q11 card footers are v1.1 per the documented corrections - file-level version should track the highest individual card version).

New builds: QB3_I - AFS Convention (scope, MEPC.331(76) cybutryne ban, paint type comparison, hydrogel technology, biocide-free class-approved paints, TBT hazard/imposex, certificate validity), examiner not yet assigned. QB1_J - hull paint coating inspection and propeller securing/locking inspection, split from a single combined WhatsApp question into 2 cards. Originally built as QB3_H/QB1_I; renamed to QB3_I/QB1_J after discovering those filenames were already live on GitHub with unrelated content (missed update from a prior session/batch) - confirmed via direct GitHub raw fetch before renaming. Both auth-gated after Nixon's review.

20 new questions from reclassified UNCLASSIFIED backlog (5 Jul 2026 batch); verified against IMO/IACS/DG Shipping sources; AECS course definition corrected (QB4_H Q1/Q5) and GBS scope limited to bulk carriers/tankers >=150m corrected (QB1_H Q5); auth-gated after Nixon's content review

Renamed from *_BACKLOG to live filenames after Nixon built and uploaded the HTML (no longer backlog). Also removed the erroneous standalone QB4_H_CheatSheet.html manifest entry - it is QB4_H's cheat sheet, referenced via the QB file's cheatsheet field, not an independent QB entry.

Session-wide correction pass triggered by two candidate comments: (1) IOPC 1992 Fund compensation limit was stale at 135m SDR platform-wide, missing the 300.74m SDR step-up tier entirely (Article 4(4)(b) trigger, top-3 contributing States >=600 million tonnes/yr) - fixed in QB1_A Q2/Q5, QB1_B Q12, QB9_A Q3; (2) SOLAS Ch. XI regulation numbers were wrong in QB4_C Q3 (SSAS mislabeled as Reg 3 instead of Reg 6; fabricated Ship Construction File under XI-1 removed per Nixon, correctly belongs to SOLAS II-1); (3) PSC deficiency action codes were wrong platform-wide - audited all 6 QB files mentioning PSC/deficiency/detention, found QB4_A and QB1_B already correct (QB4_A previously fixed 5 Jul 2026), rebuilt QB4_C Q11 and fixed QB4_E Q8 against verified current Paris MOU action-taken and deficiency-category code lists. Also fully rebuilt QB2_F Q6 (lifeboat ventilation) per IMO Res. MSC.535(107).

New derived cross-reference page bundling all 585 live QB questions by Kochi MMD examiner (Nair/Simon/Rajappan/Srivastava/Senthil/Paul), built via 3-tier examiner attribution (xlsx tracker confirmed > CE Oral Tip tag > topic-inferred). Public teaser at /SQ/ opens Paul Sir's full 10-question set free, previews 5 of Simon Sir's 212, locks the rest. Full entry + regeneration notes in notes_content_index.json under series.cross-reference-pages. ICOF — full name, who issues, contents, validity, and survey cycle.

Timber/log deck cargo — load line requirements, weight gain during voyage, and CE’s stability and securing actions.

How do the BLU Code, IMSBC Code, and the loadicator work together during bulk cargo loading?
As Chief Engineer, what are your “red lines” — situations where you will not proceed with loading or carriage regardless of commercial pressure?

QB2_B.html.title: Cargo, Ship Types & Container Fire Safety

questions.text: What documents and certificates are required when carrying IMDG cargo? Where do you find firefighting details for IMDG cargo?

How are Gross Tonnage (GT) and Net Tonnage (NT) calculated for a container ship?
What are the container test procedures? Do not limit the answer to the one-door-off test — describe the full range of structural and leakage tests carried out on containers.

What is the dry chemical powder (DCP) firefighting system fitted on the deck of a container ship, and what regulations govern it?
How is firefighting on deck carried out on a container ship when a container fire is suspected or confirmed?
How would you apply Formal Safety Assessment (FSA) specifically in the context of Li-ion battery cargo carried in containers?
What is the difference between watertight and weathertight on a ship, and what are the test methods for each?

Also corrected regulatory citations: rescue boat carriage requirement corrected from SOLAS III/21 (passenger-ship provision) to III/31 (cargo-ship provision); readiness citation corrected to III/14; embarkation/launching/recovery citation corrected to III/17; launching-appliance trim/list capability citation corrected from LSA Code §5.1.1.1 to §6.1.1.11. corrections_applied: Q6 (Totally enclosed lifeboat ventilation): full rebuild. Previous content was pre-amendment generic LSA 4.6/4.7/4.8 text with a fabricated 10-minute air-support figure. Corrected to IMO Res. MSC.535(107) (adopted 8 June 2023) - new LSA Code paras 4.6.6/4.6.7, ventilation rate 5 m3/h per person for 24h, powered-or-passive rule, fuel-source restriction, MOL Comfort (2013) casualty link, force date 1 Jan 2026, application date 1 Jan 2029 (new-build only).

corrections_applied: Q3 (SOLAS Chapter XI): corrected multiple wrong regulation numbers - SSAS is XI-2 Reg 6 (was mislabeled Reg 3, which actually covers Contracting Govt security-level obligations); removed fabricated 'AIS Security Links' (Reg 4) and 'ISSC Certification' (Reg 5) entries; ESP is XI-1 Reg 2 (was mislabeled Reg 6); removed fabricated 'Ship Construction File' from XI-1 (it is a SOLAS II-1 Reg 3-10 item, not XI-1) per Nixon's direct correction; removed stray Gemini '[cite: 1]' draft artifacts from this card.

Q11 (PSC deficiency action codes): full rebuild. Nearly all action codes were wrong - Code 30 is grounds for detention (file said Code 15/Investigation); Code 17 is rectify-before-departure/non-detainable (file said Flag/Class notified); Code 70 (RO/Class informed) was missing entirely. Verified against current Paris MOU published action-taken code list. Added distinction between 2-digit action-taken codes and 5-digit deficiency item/category codes.

Q11 (PSC deficiency action codes): governing resolution updated throughout from the superseded IMO Res. A.1185(33) to the current A.1206(34) (Procedures for Port State Control, 2025, in force 1 January 2026). Action-taken code content (30/70/17/etc.) was already correct and unchanged. Cross-linked to QB4 Q6 and QB4_E Q8 for the appeal escalation chain.

corrections_applied: Q8 (PSC appeal process): corrected conflation of action-taken codes with deficiency item/category codes, and wrong category numbers - fire safety is category 07 (file said '15-series'), ISM is category 15, MLC 2006 is category 18 (file said '18-series = Stability and Structure', which is fabricated). Verified against current Paris MOU deficiency code categories.

Q8 (PSC appeal process): Stage 3 deadline corrected from '120 days of the ship's release' (Paris MOU figure, wrongly presented as flowing from A.1206(34) itself) to the correct IOMOU figure of 90 days from the date of detention — IOMOU governs Indian ports and is the relevant regime for Kochi. Clarified that A.1206(34) itself grants only a bare right of appeal; the staged escalation mechanism (national appeal -> Flag State/RO reconsideration -> Detention Review Panel) is regional MOU procedure, not IMO text. IOMOU panel composition specified as 3 member authorities. Cross-linked to QB4 Q6 and QB4_C Q11.

corrections_applied: 2026-07-17: Q1 corrected — removed false claim that Fuel Lifecycle Label (FLL)/well-to-wake (WTW) data 'directly feeds' or is 'required to calculate' CII. CII (MARPOL Annex VI Reg 28, MEPC.354(78)) is tank-to-wake-only using fixed Cf factors on IMO DCS fuel consumption; FLL/WTW feeds FuelEU Maritime and the IMO Net-Zero Framework GFI rating — separate regulatory tracks. Fixed across 15s answer, 60s answer, Practical Application, CE Oral Tip, Reg-Box. Flagged via candidate feedback.

corrections_applied: Q2 (Fund Convention): corrected stale 135m SDR combined CLC+Fund cap to current 203m SDR (post-1 Nov 2003), added the 300.74m SDR (300,740,000 SDR) step-up tier triggered when top-3 Fund-contributing States combined receive >=600 million tonnes of contributing oil in the preceding calendar year (verified vs IOPC Funds consolidated convention text). Also fixed CE Oral Tip which had incorrectly called 203m 'a common wrong answer'.
Q5 (CLC): numbers-box also carried the stale 'Combined CLC+Fund cap = 135 million SDR' reference; updated to 203m/300.74m to match Q2.

QB1_B.html.title: Conventions & Liability — B Series

questions.text: Explain the HNS Convention — to whom does it apply, is it currently in force, and if an HNS-type pollution incident occurs where the convention is not yet in force, what is the liability/compensation fallback (LLMC limits)?
